name: OpenHunts
description: Open, community-run Product Hunt-style directory where makers launch products daily and the community upvotes them; audience is indie makers, founders and early adopters looking for new tools.

auth.notes: Create a free maker account with email/password (social sign-in often available). Submit your product, add media and a first comment, then rally upvotes on launch day.

best_practices: OpenHunts runs daily upvote rounds, so schedule your launch and notify your audience early to ride the day's leaderboard.
Write a single jargon-free tagline that explains the product to a maker scrolling a feed; clarity beats cleverness here.
Pick a small set of accurate topics/tags so the right early adopters discover you in category feeds.
Post a genuine, personal first comment as the maker explaining why you built it and what feedback you want.
Upload a crisp square logo plus 2-4 gallery screenshots or a GIF; visual products convert far better in the grid.
Engage with every comment and upvoter during the launch window; this open community rewards responsive makers.
Add your Twitter/X handle so supporters can follow and reshare your launch.

questions.label: Tagline
questions.help: One clear, jargon-free line describing what the product does.

questions.label: Description
questions.help: Short, scannable summary of what it does and who it's for.

questions.label: Topics / tags
questions.help: Pick the most relevant topics so the right audience finds you.

questions.label: Gallery images
questions.help: Screenshots or a GIF of the product in action.

questions.label: First comment (maker note)
questions.help: Introduce yourself, why you built it, and what feedback you want.
